steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what both "carves" and "leaves" mean in context. Gogol carves his name into clay and leaves his signature in brush strokes — both actions create a lasting visible impression. The correct word must fit naturally in both slots.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'marks': means makes a visible sign or impression on a surface. "He marks his name into clay" and "marks his signature in fat brush strokes" — both fit perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'passes': means moves by or transfers something. Gogol is not moving past the clay or handing off the paintings — he is creating impressions on them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'turns': means rotates or changes direction. Neither carving a name nor brushing a signature involves turning anything. ✗
  • Choice D — 'follows': means goes after something or traces a pre-existing path. Gogol is originating the marks himself, not following anything set by someone else. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
